mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate indicates accommodative monetary policy, which reduces borrowing costs and boosts equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances with FEDFUNDS < 1.5% despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a tightening labor market, which drives corporate earnings and consumer spending, resulting in positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in 40%+ of cases where PAYEMS > 150k and UNRATE < 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) and residential sales (RSAFS) indicate strong demand for housing, which stimulates economic activity and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in 35%+ of cases where HOUST > 1350k and RSAFS > 525B.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production (INDPRO) reflects robust supply-side activity, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of instances with INDPRO > 109.5.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 2% signals a tightening monetary cycle, which increases borrowing costs and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances with FEDFUNDS > 2%.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, strong GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates economic overheating, which may prompt monetary tightening and result in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 40% of instances with GDPC1 > 19100B during late-cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate below 3.5% indicates a tight labor market, which can drive wage inflation and corporate cost pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 45% of instances with UNRATE < 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of high inflation (CPIAUCSL) and low federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) signals an accommodative monetary policy amid rising prices, which may lead to market uncertainty and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 35% of cases where CPIAUCSL > 258.5 and FEDFUNDS < 1.55%.
